verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46) is a single-pass type I TM protein whose steady-state localization is the trans-Golgi network, but it constitutively cycles between the TGN and the plasma membrane via CARTS (PKD-dependent TGN-to-PM carriers), returning via early endosomes. The cytoplasmic YQRL motif drives PM internalization and TGN retrieval, keeping the dominant pool intracellular. Nevertheless, a real PM pool is present at steady state via the protein's own TM anchor and trafficking mechanism — not via recruitment by another surface protein. This is classic dual_localization cycling between an endomembrane compartment and the PM.
